checks.name: lint

patternCompliance.pattern: Bootstrap files use sys.path.insert for .cnogo prefix
All path references migrated from scripts/ and docs/templates/ to .cnogo/
install.sh uses block markers for gitignore management
Settings merge uses _cnogo marker for hook ownership tracking

performanceFindings.description: generate_manifest() spawns Python per-file for sha256. Acceptable for ~126 files at install time, not a hot path.
performanceFindings.recommendation: Consider batch sha256 computation if file count grows significantly

principleNotes.note: All 4 plans verified. 406 tests pass. Self-host detection works correctly. cnogo-init.md stale reference fixed during review.
No OWASP violations. Install.sh operates on controlled local paths. No secrets in code. Settings merge uses safe Python JSON parsing.
All PLAN/SUMMARY/CONTEXT artifacts present. Memory phase progression coherent. No worker closure violations.
No hot-path concerns. Install/update runs once. Python-per-file sha256 is acceptable for installer scope.
install.sh well-structured with named functions. Bootstrap pattern is clean and consistent across scripts/hooks.
406 Python tests pass. install.sh lifecycle operations (update, uninstall, force) verified manually via plan verification commands but lack automated test suite.
Changes surgical across 4 plans. cnogo-init.md fix is a legitimate missed reference from Plan 02 migration, not a drive-by refactor.

securityFindings.description: Theoretical path injection in manifest JSON generation via filenames containing quotes. Mitigated: paths are from controlled local filesystem, not user input.

evidence: Plans 01-04 goals match actual diff: path migration, reference updates, install.sh rewrite, self-host verification
114 files changed, +2660/-269 — proportional to 4-plan packaging migration
All CONTEXT/PLAN/SUMMARY artifacts present with correct schemaVersion
stageReviews.notes: All 4 plans match stated goals. Artifact contracts complete. One stale reference in cnogo-init.md fixed during review.

evidence: Self-host install succeeds: 126 files tracked in manifest

findings.description: install.sh bash functions lack automated test suite — verified via manual plan verification commands only
findings.recommendation: Consider adding installer integration tests in a future plan

stageReviews.notes: Code quality strong. Security acceptable for installer scope. One concern: install.sh test coverage is manual only.
